trajectory.description: Observation: Runtime error - Conv2d channel mismatch in STConvBlock. The first temporal conv had in_channels=32 but input only has 1 channel. Analysis: The STConvBlock was not properly passing in_channels to the first temporal conv. Decision: Rewrite model.py with correct channel flow: input (1ch) -> temporal1 (1->hidden) -> graph_conv (hidden) -> temporal2 (hidden->out) + residual. Also improve train.py and predict.py for robustness.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline STGCN trained for 10 epochs, val MAE=3.3746. Training loss still decreasing but val MAE fluctuating. Model has only 200K params. Analysis: Key improvements needed: (1) Per-sensor normalization instead of global - different sensors have very different speed distributions, (2) More training epochs with early stopping, (3) Larger hidden dim for better capacity, (4) Huber loss for robustness. Decision: Implement all improvements - per-sensor normalization, hidden_dim=256, Huber loss, 50 epochs with patience=10 early stopping, warmup + cosine schedule. Also submit current baseline eval to get test score.

trajectory.description: Observation: STGCN trained for 36 epochs (early stopped), val MAE=3.1791. No eval submitted yet - need to get test score baseline. Analysis: Current architecture is basic; GWNet-style architecture with dilated causal convolutions + adaptive adjacency typically achieves much better results on METR-LA. Decision: Submit eval for current checkpoint to get baseline test score, then implement improved architecture next iteration.

Observation: Evaluation failed due to torch.load weights_only=True default in PyTorch 2.6+. The checkpoint contains numpy arrays which can't be loaded with weights_only=True. Analysis: Need to add weights_only=False to torch.load in predict.py. Also, the current STGCN architecture (val MAE 3.18) can be improved - Graph WaveNet with dilated causal convolutions, adaptive adjacency, and mix-hop propagation typically achieves much better results on METR-LA. Decision: Fix predict.py, implement GWNet architecture, and retrain.

Observation: GWNet trained for 50 epochs, val MAE=3.27, mae60≈3.70. No eval submitted yet (previous eval failed due to weights_only issue, now fixed). Analysis: Key improvements needed: (1) RevIN for handling distribution shifts in time series, (2) larger model capacity (hidden_dim=64→128), (3) Huber loss (more robust than MSE), (4) early stopping on mae_60min (target metric), (5) higher dropout (0.2) for larger model regularization. Decision: Implement all improvements and train. Also submit eval for current checkpoint to get baseline test score.

trajectory.description: Observation: GWNet trained to best val mae60=3.7710 (epoch 32), early stopped at epoch 52. No eval submitted yet - previous eval failed on weights_only issue which is now fixed. Need to get test score baseline before making improvements. Analysis: The gap between overall MAE (3.10) and mae60 (3.77) is significant, suggesting the model struggles with longer horizons. Decision: Submit eval for current checkpoint to establish baseline test score, then plan targeted improvements for mae_60min.

Observation: Test mae_60min=4.1124 vs val mae_60min=3.77 (9% gap). The double normalization (z-score + RevIN) is likely hurting - RevIN on z-scored data removes useful per-sensor distributional info. Analysis: Key issues: (1) RevIN + z-score double normalization, (2) Overfitting gap between val and test, (3) Too aggressive horizon weighting (6x) may cause instability. Decision: Remove RevIN, increase regularization (dropout 0.3, weight_decay 3e-4), moderate horizon weights (max 4x), slightly larger model (hidden_dim=160), train longer (100 epochs patience 25).

Previous test mae_60min=4.1124 with val mae60=3.77. Time remaining ~42 min. Analysis: The val mae60 improved by 0.14, but the test-val gap was 0.34 last time. Need to submit eval to see if test improved proportionally. If test gap persists, consider stronger regularization or different approaches. Decision: Submit eval for current checkpoint immediately to get test score, then plan next steps based on remaining time.

Observation: Test mae_60min=4.1193 vs best=4.1124. The val-test gap widened (0.34→0.48) with the larger model, indicating overfitting/distribution shift. Analysis: RevIN normalizes each window independently, handling distribution shift. Combined with a smaller model (hidden_dim=64), this should reduce the val-test gap. Decision: Implement RevIN (no z-score), smaller model, train 100 epochs.

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 862s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: RevIN+hidden_dim=64 gave val mae60=4.24 (worse than z-score best of 3.77). Best test mae_60min=4.1124 with z-score+hidden_dim=128+Huber loss. Analysis: Z-score normalization works much better than RevIN for this task. The key remaining improvement is to use L1 loss directly in raw space (since MAE is the evaluation metric) instead of Huber/MSE in normalized space. With ~14min left, I can train ~25-30 epochs with hidden_dim=128. Decision: Retrain with z-score, hidden_dim=128, L1 loss in raw space, no horizon weighting, higher lr for faster convergence, then submit eval.
